# Several sesam repos can coexist in one git repo. Their git-config subsections
# (and the .gitattributes driver names) are suffixed with the encoded relative
# sesam dir, so a second `sesam init` does not clobber the first repo's drivers.
# (Hooks use the same suffix, so on git >= 2.54 both repos' hooks are installed
# side by side rather than overwriting each other.)

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

mkdir sub-a
mkdir sub-b
exec sesam init --user admin --sesam-dir sub-a
exec sesam init --user admin --sesam-dir sub-b

# Each repo keeps its own diff-driver subsection, carrying its own --sesam-dir.
exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff-sub-a.textconv
stdout '^sesam --sesam-dir=sub-a show$'
exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff-sub-b.textconv
stdout '^sesam --sesam-dir=sub-b show$'

# The generic, unsuffixed subsection must not exist when repos are nested.
! exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv

# Each .gitattributes references only its own suffixed drivers.
grep 'diff=sesam-diff-sub-a' sub-a/.gitattributes
! grep 'diff=sesam-diff-sub-b' sub-a/.gitattributes
grep 'diff=sesam-diff-sub-b' sub-b/.gitattributes

# The alias is a plain shim, shared and harmless across repos.
exec git config --local alias.sesam
stdout '^!sesam$'
